provisions.text: Located primarily on the city's main arterial roads, linear Growth Corridors (GC) form corridors of development that are prime opportunities for increasing residential densities and supporting existing commercial uses. These areas are also ideal for mixed use development due to the proximity to transit and Active transportation linkages along the arterials. Growth corridors should support walkable neighbourhoods by encouraging street related buildings with parking on the sides or rear yards.
Rezoning from GC to GN may be considered where the policies of the Official Plan can be demonstrated.
The following uses are permitted in the GC Zone:
Cemetery
Commercial Daycare Centre
Community Care Facility
Community Institutional
Cultural Establishment
Dormitory
Educational Institution (private)
Educational Institution (public)
Fire Station
Funeral Establishment
Government Offices
Hospital
Library
Medical, Health and Dental Office
Place of Worship
Police Station
Public Utility Service Operation
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
Automobile Sales and Services
Automobile Service Station
Cannabis Retail Store
Convenience Store
Drive-thru Business
Eating and Drinking Establishment
Entertainment Establishment
Equipment Sales, Rental Service
Garden Centre
Greenhouse (nursery retail)
Grocery Store
Hostel / Hotel
Laundromat
Office
Outdoor Retail Display
Personal Service Shop
Retail Store
Retail Store with connected Retail Warehouse, Light Manufacturing or assembly facility
Retail Warehouse
Service Repair Establishment
Shopping Centre
Theatre
Transportation Service
Veterinary Services
Warehouse, Storage Facility and/or Distribution Centre
Parking Lot / Structure
Transport Terminal
These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met:
Lot Area N/A
Frontage min. 12 m
Front Yard Setback min. 2 m max.6 m the front yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
Flanking Yard Setback min. 2 m the flankage yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 20% of the streetline
Side Yard Setback min. 0 m, 6 m bordering residential zone
Rear Yard Setback min. 12 m when abutting RN, or RM, otherwise 6 m
Coverage max. 50%, 70% if half the parking is located underground
Drive-through's will only be considered by Development Agreement.
Gas stations will only be considered by Development Agreement.
Single storey chain stores will be considered by development agreement.
Flaglots are permitted with no less than 6m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 22.3.2 for both the flaglot and the main lot.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.9 requirements are met.
One primary building per lot is permitted.

Attached garages shall not front onto the street frontage unless it provides access to an underground parking garage that does not exceed 25% of the primary building frontage on any streetline to a maximum width of 6 m.

All street-facing facades shall provide at least one horizontal or vertical facade articulation of no less than 300 mm for no less than 30% of the facade area. 2.5 m 4 storeys Sloping Street Streetline Grade
Streetwall height varies depending on the allowable height of the building as follows:
If the allowable building height is equal to or less than 5 storeys, the maximum allowable streetwall height shall be 3 storeys
If the allowable building height is 6 storeys, the maximum allowable streetwall height shall be 4 storeys
Streetwall height can be less than the maximum allowable streetwall height to a minimum of 2 storeys high.
Streetwall heights can step up or down along sloping streetline grades as long as the maximum height is not exceeded (see Figure 22.1).
Building height is controlled by lot depth and side yard adjacency such that:
Any GC zoned property that borders a residential zone on its side yard shall have a maximum building height of 4 storeys.
Any GC zoned property that borders a residential zone on its rear yard that has an average lot depth less than 38 m shall have a maximum building height of 4 storeys;

Any GC zoned property that borders a residential zone on its rear yard that has an average lot Depth greater than 38 m Avg. Lot Depth < 38 m = 4 Storeys; but less than 60 m shall have a maximum building height of 5 storeys
Any GC zoned property that borders a residential zone on its rear yard that has an average lot depth that exceeds 60 m shall have a maximum building height of 6 storeys.

Any GC zoned property that shares side yards and rear yard with another GC zoned property shall have a maximum building height of 6 storeys. The properties shall have been in existence at the time this bylaw was adopted.
The maximum building dimension is 60 m.
Parking is not permitted between the street and the building.
Primary building lobbies shall front onto the street-front.
The amenity requirements in Section 3.14 shall be followed.

For multi-unit buildings, each grade-related unit shall include a patio door. 2.5 m for 70% of streetwall Min. Side Yard Setback 0 m, 6 m bordering residential zone Max. Lot Coverage 50%, 70% if half the parking is located underground
All driveways shall be hard surfaced with asphalt, concrete or unit pavers. Loose gravel or soil is not permitted.
Only one driveway per 20 m of frontage is permitted to a maximum of two (2) driveways per lot.
Shared driveways are encouraged between neighbouring properties whenever possible.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced pathway shall link the parking lot to the front door of every building.
A minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced pathway shall link the sidewalk to the front door of every building.

If there are no street trees in the bordering public street right-of-way, each development shall include no less than 1 tree per 10 m of frontage between the building and the street. Decimal results shall be rounded up.
A 3m landscape buffer is required for any GC properties that border residential zoned properties in the rear yard. Each landscape buffer shall include:
A 1.8 m high opaque fence along the rear yard
One tree per 6 m of rear yard border
Shrub beds at a rate of 2 m2 per tree consisting of shrubs, annuals or perennials.
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
Each grade-related unit must provide no less than 4 m2 of private outdoor amenity space (patio, balcony, rooftop deck, or small yard) to ensure resident comfort and privacy.
Surface parking lots that exceed 24 cars shall include one landscaped island per 15 parking spots. Decimal results shall be rounded up.

shared_requirement_references.source_text: Rezoning from GC to GN may be considered where the policies of the Official Plan can be demonstrated.
Flaglots are permitted with no less than 6m frontage as long as all other site and building requirements are met in Section 22.3.2 for both the flaglot and the main lot.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Home occupations are permitted if Section 4.9 requirements are met.
The amenity requirements in Section 3.14 shall be followed.

The zone contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
